Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Lillian K. Sing; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

No appearances.

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DENISE BATTISTA's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Where the sufficiency of the proposed amendment is a novel question almost certain to be tested in an appellate court, the preferable practice is to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer or other appropriate motion. (California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281.) Moreover, ordinarily, the judge will not consider the validity of the proposed amended pleading in deciding whether to grant leave to amend; rather, after leave is granted, the opposing party will have the opportunity to attack the validity of the amended pleading. (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Marker, U.S.A.) (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) Finally, Defendants have not established that they will be unduly prejudiced by Plaintiff's proposed amendment. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
